---
title: "Forward-looking α"
subtitle: "GRAP 104 provisioning model"
author: "Probity Data Analytics"
date: today
format: probity-slides-typst
# Footer text (all optional):
footer-text: "Probity Data Analytics"        # brand, bold navy (left); omit → default
footer-note: "Confidential"                  # classification after the brand; remove this line to hide
footer-center: "GRAP 104 forward-looking α"  # running title (footer centre); omit → none
---

## Agenda

- Key finding and executive summary
- Analysis and supporting evidence
- Scenario comparison and recommendations

```{=typst}
#prob-section("Part One", subtitle: "The Model")
```

```{=typst}
#prob-navy-slide("The model", subtitle: "A linear regression on four post-COVID fiscal years, with the inflation-growth gap lagged one year.")[
  #prob-equation-box[Loss Rate#sub[t] = β#sub[0] + β#sub[1] · (Inflation-Growth Gap)#sub[t−1] + ε]
  #v(0.5cm)
  #prob-metric-cards((
    (label: "Intercept",       value: [β#sub[0] = +0.0861],  desc: [Baseline loss rate when the inflation-growth gap is zero.]),
    (label: "Macro slope",     value: [β#sub[1] = +0.00862], desc: [Positive sign matches theory. Stress up, losses up.]),
    (label: "Goodness of fit", value: [R#super[2] = 0.97],   desc: [Post-COVID fit (n = 4); the COVID year is excluded.]),
  ))
]
```

## Computing α Each Year

At year-end, read the two macro series for the year just ended; apply the formula; multiply every matrix cell by α.

```{=typst}
#prob-cols(
  prob-steps((
    (title: [Read the macros], body: [SARB real-GDP growth (KBP6006C) and Stats SA headline CPI YoY (CPI60001), for the fiscal year just ended.]),
    (title: [Form the inflation-growth gap], body: [ig_gap is the simple subtraction: how far inflation ran ahead of growth.]),
    (title: [Compute α], body: [Apply the published coefficients, scale to the mean loss rate, then the neutral band and clip.]),
    (title: [Apply to the matrix], body: [Multiply every cell of the provision matrix by α. Structure unchanged; only scaled.]),
  )),
  prob-formula-card(
    (
      ([ig_gap],         [cpi_yoy − gdp_growth]),
      ([predicted_loss], [0.0861 + 0.00862 × ig_gap]),
      ([α_raw],          [predicted_loss / 0.1215]),
      ([α],              [MIN(1.45, MAX(0.85, α_raw))]),
    ),
    label: "The Formula",
    note: [Excel-style; substitute the names for last year's values.],
    footnote: [Constants · 0.0861 = β₀ · 0.00862 = β₁ · 0.1215 = mean loss rate · [0.85, 1.45] = clip],
    height: 7.7cm,
  ),
  ratio: (1fr, 1.1fr),
)
```

## How α Responds Across Scenarios

Four illustrative macro states. The neutral band holds α at 1 around the baseline; the clip stops it running away in either direction.

```{=typst}
#prob-scenario-cards((
  (accent: probity-green,    label: "Strong Growth", meta: [GDP +3 % · CPI 3 % · gap 0],  value: [α = 0.85], desc: [Below the neutral band; α held at its 0.85 floor.]),
  (accent: probity-mid-blue, label: "Steady",        meta: [GDP +1 % · CPI 5 % · gap 4],  value: [α = 1.00], desc: [Inside the neutral band; matrix is left unchanged.]),
  (accent: probity-gold,     label: "Downturn",      meta: [GDP −1 % · CPI 7 % · gap 8],  value: [α = 1.28], desc: [Stagflation. Matrix lifted by about 28 %.]),
  (accent: probity-red,      label: "Severe Stress", meta: [GDP −3 % · CPI 10 % · gap 13],value: [α = 1.45], desc: [Above the band; α held at its 1.45 cap.]),
))
```

## Worked Example: FY 2024/25

```{=typst}
#prob-cols(
  table(
    columns: (auto, 1fr, auto),
    align: (center + horizon, left + horizon, right + horizon),
    stroke: none,
    fill: (_, row) => if row == 0 { probity-navy } else if calc.odd(row) { probity-pale-tint } else { white },
    inset: (x: 9pt, y: 7pt),
    table.header(
      text(fill: white, weight: "bold")[Step],
      text(fill: white, weight: "bold")[Quantity],
      text(fill: white, weight: "bold")[Value],
    ),
    [1], [FY24/25 real-GDP growth], [+0.70 %],
    [1], [FY24/25 CPI YoY],         [+3.23 %],
    [2], [ig_gap = 3.23 − 0.70],    [+2.53],
    [3], [predicted_loss],          [10.79 %],
    [3], [α_raw = 10.79 / 12.15],   [0.888],
    [4], text(fill: probity-gold, weight: "bold")[Apply to matrix], text(fill: probity-gold, weight: "bold")[× 0.89],
  ),
  prob-result-card(
    [α = 0.89],
    label: "Result",
    desc: [Inflation eased while growth stayed weak, so the gap sat below baseline.],
    footnote: [The matrix scales down by about 11 %.],
    height: 7.7cm,
  ),
  ratio: (1.35fr, 1fr),
)
```

## Indicator Selection

A branded data table (`prob-data-table`) — navy header, pale-tint zebra rows, horizon-aligned cells. Body cells are raw content, so the status column can carry colour.

```{=typst}
#prob-data-table(
  columns: (1fr, auto, 2.4fr),
  align: (left + horizon, center + horizon, left + horizon),
  header: ([Indicator], [Keep], [Rationale]),
  rows: (
    ([Inflation-growth gap, lagged one year], text(fill: probity-green, weight: "bold")[✓], [Single best predictor of loss-rate movement.]),
    ([Unemployment rate],                    text(fill: probity-red,   weight: "bold")[✗], [Adds noise; fails out-of-sample.]),
    ([Credit-GDP gap],                       text(fill: probity-gold,  weight: "bold")[~], [Marginally useful; folded into the gap.]),
  ),
)
```

## Auditor Questions

A Q&A block (`prob-qa`) stacks bold-navy questions over body answers.

```{=typst}
#prob-qa((
  ([Is α forward-looking as GRAP 104 §5.32 requires?], [Yes. It depends only on the prior year's published macro series, available before year-end.]),
  ([Could α move the matrix the wrong way?],            [Only outside the [0.85, 1.45] clip; inside the neutral band α is held at 1.]),
))
```

## Pipeline

A horizontal flow diagram (`prob-flow`) alternates pale-tint and navy boxes joined by gold arrows.

```{=typst}
#prob-flow((
  (title: [Inputs],  body: [SARB GDP, Stats SA CPI for the year just ended.]),
  (title: [Engine],  body: [Compute the inflation-growth gap and apply α.]),
  (title: [Outputs], body: [Every cell of the provision matrix, scaled by α.]),
))
```
